answer: The September 15, 2026 Florida escrow reconciliation overstatement for FNF National Title closings was caused by settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joining party_identity_bridge on normalized_tax_id, which matched multiple buyer and seller party roles within the same closing, duplicating escrow disbursements in finance.disbursement_recon_daily. The root cause was using the wrong join key; the correct key is closing_party_role_id. Remediation involved switching the transform to the correct join key, quarantining normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfilling the finance.disbursement_recon_daily table for Florida purchase closings.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Switch the transform to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.root_cause: settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joined party_identity_bridge by normalized_tax_id, matching multiple buyer and seller party roles within the same closing, duplicating escrow disbursements
